saints.en: Saturday before the Nativity. St. Martyr Boniface

rubrics:
Вечірня: катизма чергова. На «Господи, взиваю» 6 стихир: 3 мч. і 3 Октоїха, Сл., І нині: догмат 4-го гл. Прокімен дня. Стиховня Октоїха. По «Отче наш»: тропар мч., Сл., І нині: богород. воскрес за гласом тр. мч. Відпуст великий, з помин. цілого тижня і мч.
Утреня: по “Бог Господь”: тропар мч. (2), Сл., І нині: богород. з воскрес. за гласом тр. мч. Катизми черг., сідал. Октоїха. Пс. 50. Канон: якщо Господній або Богородичний храм – канон храму, мч. та 1-й Октоїха. Якщо храм святого - канон мч., храмового святого та 1-й Октоїха. Катавасія тільки по 3-ій, 6-ій, 8-ій і 9-ій пісні – ірмос канону Октоїха. По 3-ій – сідал. мч. По 6-ій – конд.-ікос мч.; по 9-ій – світил. мч., Сл.: світил. Октоїха, І нині: його богород. Стиховня Октоїха. По “Отче наш”: тропар мч., Сл., І нині: богород. відпустовий за гласом тр. мч. і за днем тижня. Відпуст великий з помин. усього тижня і мч.
Часи: на 1-му – дня; на 6-му – храму; на 3-му і 9-му – мч.
Літургія: вся служба – дня. Суботи перед Різдвом: Ап. – Гал. 205 зач.; 3, 8-12. Єв. – Лк. 72 зач.; 13, 18-29.

troparion_en.text:
(Podoben: “Be quick to anticipate...”)
Fervently, you followed the renowned Martyrs /  steadfastly confessing Christ before unbelievers, O wise Boniface.  /  You returned your relics to the august Aglaϊa /   as an imperishable treasure; /   from them flow healing and mercy for the world.

kontakion_en.text:
(Podoben: “Be quick to anticipate...”)
You offered yourself voluntarily as a blameless sacrifice  /  to Him Who is about to be born of the Virgin for your sake,  /  O Saint, and wise crown-bearer Boniface.

matched_oca_saint: Martyr Boniface at Tarsus in Cilicia, and Righteous Aglaϊa of Rome

oca_saints.name: Martyr Boniface at Tarsus in Cilicia, and Righteous Aglaϊa of Rome

troparion.text:
(Podoben: “Be quick to anticipate...”)
Fervently, you followed the renowned Martyrs /  steadfastly confessing Christ before unbelievers, O wise Boniface.  /  You returned your relics to the august Aglaϊa /   as an imperishable treasure; /   from them flow healing and mercy for the world.

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “Be quick to anticipate...”)
You offered yourself voluntarily as a blameless sacrifice  /  to Him Who is about to be born of the Virgin for your sake,  /  O Saint, and wise crown-bearer Boniface.

oca_saints.name: Venerable Elias of Murom, Wonderworker of the Kiev Near Caves

troparion.text: Today the Russian land rejoices,  / glorifying its warrior and wonderworker Elias of Muromets,  / for he abides in the Church Triumphant,  / and is its kinsman in the Church Militant, /  asking the Lord for help against the visible and invisible enemies of Holy Russia,  / so that the Russian people may glorify and honor Christ God without hindrance.

kontakion.text: As a great luminary of the Russian land,  / you appeared as an invincible commander of the Heavenly King,  / and divinely inspired adornment of monastics.  / And now, standing with the Angels before the Throne of God,  / entreat the Master Christ, the Creator of all,  / that we may be delivered from all troubles and sorrows,  / that those who honor your holy memory may cry to you: “Rejoice, Venerable Elias, our Father.”

oca_saints.name: Martyrs Elias, Probus, and Ares in Cilicia

troparion.text: Your holy martyrs Elias, Probus, and Ares, O Lord, / through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. / For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through their intercessions, save our souls!

oca_saints.name: Saint Boniface the Merciful, Bishop of Ferentino

troparion.text: In truth you were revealed to your flock as a rule of faith, / an image of humility and a teacher of abstinence; / your humility exalted you; / your poverty enriched you. / Hierarch Father Boniface, / entreat Christ our God / that our souls may be saved.

In truth you were revealed to your flock as a rule of faith, / an image of humility and a teacher of abstinence; / your humility exalted you; / your poverty enriched you. / Hierarch Father Gregory, / entreat Christ our God / that our souls may be saved.

oca_saints.name: Venerable George the Scribe, and Venerable Savva, of Khakhuli

troparion.text: By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy fathers George and Savva, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!
